agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, promotions, and post-sale lifecycle. The domain enforces inventory integrity at checkout time (not cart time), time-boxed reservations, mutual exclusivity of discount mechanisms, order immutability after payment, and a time-bound refund policy.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout bounded context, with touchpoints into Inventory, Promotions/Pricing, and Returns/Refunds contexts.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient collection of desired items for a customer. Adding items to the cart does NOT reserve inventory."},{"term":"Checkout","definition":"The process initiated when a customer commits to purchasing cart contents. Triggers inventory reservation."},{"term":"Inventory Reservation","definition":"A time-limited hold on inventory units, created when checkout begins and released if payment is not completed within 15 minutes."},{"term":"Reservation Timeout","definition":"The 15-minute window after checkout begins within which payment must be confirmed; expiry releases the reserved inventory."},{"term":"Promotion","definition":"A pricing mechanism applied to a product (e.g., sale price, percentage off). A product may have at most one active promotion at any time."},{"term":"Discount Code","definition":"A customer-entered code that applies a discount to an order. Mutually exclusive with promotional pricing on the same order."},{"term":"Payment Confirmed","definition":"The terminal state transition after which an order becomes immutable. No modifications are permitted."},{"term":"Active Promotion","definition":"A promotion currently in effect for a product. Only one may exist per product at any given time."},{"term":"Refund Window","definition":"The 30-day period following delivery during which a customer may request a refund."},{"term":"Listed Product","definition":"A product visible in the catalog. Visibility does not imply purchasability; zero-inventory listed products cannot be purchased."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Adding items to the cart does not reserve inventory. Inventory is only reserved when checkout begins.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If inventory were reserved at cart-add time, the system would allow phantom stock locks, corrupting availability for all other customers. Cart-level reservation would break inventory integrity.","testable_assertion":"After a customer adds an item to their cart, the available inventory count for that item remains unchanged."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"If payment is not completed within 15 minutes of checkout initiation, the inventory reservation is released.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 15-minute window is a business policy decision. Operations could change this to 10 or 20 minutes without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"Given an inventory reservation created at time T, if no payment is confirmed by T+15 minutes, the reserved units are returned to available inventory."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A product can have at most one active promotion at a time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Multiple simultaneous promotions on a single product would create ambiguous pricing — the system cannot determine which price to display or charge, corrupting price integrity.","testable_assertion":"At any point in time, querying active promotions for a given product returns zero or one result, never more."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Discount codes cannot be combined with promotional pricing. Only one discount mechanism applies per order.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing stacking of discount mechanisms would produce prices not sanctioned by the business, violating pricing integrity and potentially causing revenue loss below approved thresholds.","testable_assertion":"When an order includes a product under active promotion, applying a discount code is rejected (or vice versa). The order total reflects exactly one discount mechanism or none."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Orders cannot be modified after payment is confirmed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Post-payment modification would break the financial record — the payment amount would no longer match the order contents, corrupting transactional integrity and potentially violating payment processing contracts.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to add, remove, or change items/quantities on an order with status 'payment confirmed' or later is rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A product with zero inventory cannot be purchased, even if it remains listed in the catalog.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing purchase of zero-inventory items would create orders that can never be fulfilled, corrupting order integrity and violating the physical constraint that you cannot ship what you do not have.","testable_assertion":"When checkout is attempted for a product with available inventory = 0, the checkout is rejected with an out-of-stock error."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refunds must be requested within 30 days of delivery.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 30-day window is a business policy. Management could extend or shorten it (e.g., holiday return policy) without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A refund request submitted more than 30 days after the recorded delivery date is rejected. A refund request submitted within 30 days is accepted for processing."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has items in their cart","when":"The customer views their cart (without starting checkout)","then":"The inventory count for those items is not reduced and other customers can still purchase them"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer initiates checkout for a product with available inventory >= requested quantity","when":"Checkout begins","then":"The requested quantity is reserved (subtracted from available inventory) and a 15-minute reservation timer starts"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer has an active inventory reservation","when":"15 minutes elapse without payment confirmation","then":"The reservation is released and the reserved units are returned to available inventory"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A customer has an active inventory reservation","when":"Payment is confirmed within 15 minutes","then":"The reservation converts to a confirmed order and inventory is permanently decremented"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A product already has an active promotion","when":"An administrator attempts to add a second promotion to the same product","then":"The system rejects the second promotion and returns an error indicating only one active promotion is allowed"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer's order includes a product with an active promotion applied","when":"The customer attempts to apply a discount code","then":"The system rejects the discount code and informs the customer that promotional pricing and discount codes cannot be combined"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A customer has applied a discount code to their order","when":"The customer adds a product that has an active promotion","then":"The system applies only one discount mechanism (either the code or the promotion) and clearly communicates which applies"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An order has payment confirmed","when":"Any attempt is made to modify the order (add/remove/change items)","then":"The modification is rejected and the order remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A product is listed in the catalog with available inventory = 0","when":"A customer attempts to purchase the product","then":"The purchase is blocked with a clear out-of-stock message"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An order was delivered 29 days ago","when":"The customer requests a refund","then":"The refund request is accepted for processing"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An order was delivered 31 days ago","when":"The customer requests a refund","then":"The refund request is rejected with a message that the 30-day refund window has expired"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"Two customers have the same item in their carts and only one unit remains in inventory","when":"Both customers initiate checkout simultaneously","then":"Only one customer successfully reserves the unit; the other receives an out-of-stock error"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer adds item to cart, item goes to zero inventory before checkout","expected_behavior":"Checkout is rejected with an out-of-stock error. The cart may still display the item but it cannot proceed to reservation."},{"scenario":"Inventory reservation expires at exactly the 15-minute boundary while payment is being processed","expected_behavior":"The system must handle this atomically. If payment confirmation arrives at the exact boundary, a clear tiebreaker rule is needed (recommend: reservation must be valid at the moment payment is confirmed)."},{"scenario":"Product promotion is deactivated while a customer is mid-checkout with promotional pricing","expected_behavior":"The price locked at checkout initiation should be honored for that session. Alternatively, the business must decide whether to re-price at payment confirmation."},{"scenario":"Customer applies discount code, then product gets a promotion added before payment","expected_behavior":"The discount mechanism chosen at checkout time should be preserved. The mutual exclusivity rule must be enforced based on order state at the time of each action."},{"scenario":"Refund requested on exactly day 30 after delivery","expected_behavior":"The request should be accepted — 'within 30 days' includes day 30 itself. Boundary should be inclusive."},{"scenario":"Partial inventory available (customer wants 5, only 3 in stock)","expected_behavior":"Spec does not address partial fulfillment. Expected behavior: reject the checkout for the full quantity, or offer partial fulfillment. Needs business decision."},{"scenario":"Multiple discount codes entered sequentially","expected_behavior":"Only one discount code should apply per order (consistent with 'one discount mechanism' rule). Subsequent codes replace or are rejected."},{"scenario":"Customer abandons checkout, reservation expires, then returns to complete payment","expected_behavior":"Payment must fail because reservation no longer exists. Customer must re-initiate checkout, which will attempt a new reservation against current inventory."},{"scenario":"Order delivered but delivery date is disputed by customer","expected_behavior":"The 30-day refund window start date depends on recorded delivery date. If delivery date is contested, a dispute resolution process is needed (not specified)."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Inventory reservation with timeout prevents overselling (revenue protection) while avoiding indefinite stock locks that block legitimate sales. Discount mutual exclusivity prevents unintended margin erosion from stacked discounts. The 30-day refund window bounds financial exposure from returns.","operational":"Lazy reservation (at checkout, not cart) maximizes inventory liquidity and simplifies stock management. The 15-minute timeout keeps reservation churn manageable. Single-promotion-per-product eliminates pricing ambiguity for catalog and checkout systems.","user":"Customers see real-time availability (no phantom locks from carts), get clear pricing (one discount mechanism), and have a known refund window. The tradeoff is that items in the cart are not guaranteed — a customer could lose an item between cart and checkout.","risk":"Primary risk is the race condition at checkout when multiple customers compete for the last unit. The 15-minute timeout creates a secondary risk where slow payment processors could cause legitimate purchases to lose their reservation. Post-payment immutability means any order errors require a refund-and-reorder flow rather than in-place correction.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"No cart-level guarantee of item availability; clear discount rules; firm 30-day refund boundary; no post-payment order edits"},{"group":"Product/Merchandising Team","impact":"Must manage one promotion per product constraint; cannot stack promotions for aggressive sales events without removing the prior promotion first"},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Order immutability after payment simplifies reconciliation; bounded refund window limits open liabilities; single discount mechanism per order simplifies revenue accounting"},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Reservation timeout ensures inventory is not locked indefinitely; zero-inventory purchase block prevents unfulfillable orders from entering the pipeline"},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must handle edge cases: expired reservations, refund window disputes, discount code rejections when promotions are active, post-payment modification requests that must be denied"}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The specification is clear, internally consistent, and covers the major lifecycle events (cart, checkout, payment, refund). All seven stated rules are unambiguous and non-contradictory. Confidence is not higher because several operational details are unspecified (partial fulfillment, price locking during checkout, timezone for refund window, boundary behavior at 15 minutes).","high_confidence_areas":["Inventory reservation lifecycle (cart vs checkout vs payment)","Discount mutual exclusivity rule","Post-payment order immutability","Zero-inventory purchase block","Refund window policy"],"low_confidence_areas":["Partial fulfillment behavior when requested quantity exceeds available inventory","Price locking semantics — whether promotional price at checkout start is honored if promotion changes mid-session","Exact boundary behavior at 15-minute reservation timeout (inclusive vs exclusive)","Timezone and exact time-of-day semantics for the 30-day refund window","Whether 'per order' discount exclusivity applies at order level or line-item level","Dispute resolution when delivery date (refund window start) is contested"],"assumptions":["The 15-minute reservation timeout is measured from checkout initiation, not from some other event","'Within 30 days' is inclusive of day 30","Discount mutual exclusivity applies at the order level, not per line item","Inventory counts are managed as integer units","Payment confirmation is a discrete, atomic event"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, promotions, and post-sale lifecycle. The domain enforces inventory integrity at checkout time (not cart time), time-boxed reservations, mutual exclusivity of discount mechanisms, order immutability after payment, and a time-bound refund policy.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout bounded context, with touchpoints into Inventory, Promotions/Pricing, and Returns/Refunds contexts.","u

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this e-commerce domain scenario carefully.

The rules are clear and well-defined. Let me check for any issues:

1. Cart doesn't reserve inventory - clear
2. Checkout reserves inventory, 15-min timeout - clear
3. Max one active promotion per product - clear
4. Discount codes can't combine with promotional pricing - clear
5. Orders immutable after payment confirmed - clear
6. Zero inventory = can't purchase even if listed - clear
7. Refunds within 30 days of delivery - clear

No contradictions. No real ambiguity. The spec is reasonably complete for what it states. 

While there might be some edge cases like partial inventory handling, these aren't significant enough to warrant escalation. The specification is internally consistent and clear on the core requirements, so I can move forward with confidence.
